top_risk: Karma collapses into a second activity counter (or a collusion/alt-farmed number), so it duplicates XP instead of measuring a distinct 'appreciation' axis.
cheapest_evidence: Run a time-boxed manual/thin-slice trial and correlate per-member karma against XP rank; low correlation plus a plausible top-N shows the axis is distinct and not trivially gamed.
report:
## (1) Core claim
Peer-granted reputation score positioned as a third axis alongside XP (activity) and coins (economy) with its own leaderboard. Load-bearing claim is narrow/testable: person-to-person appreciation is meaningfully distinct from and additive to activity/economy.
## (2) Strongest failure mode
Degeneration into a correlated duplicate: (a) activity-collinearity (most active = most seen = most karma; karma rank approx XP rank), (b) gaming (collusion/mutual-boosting/sybil/alt). Rate-limiting/caps/sybil resistance not measured. Spendability not measured; if spendable, gaming incentive rises.
## (3) Falsification / cheapest evidence
Falsifier: karma not distinct or not trustworthy. Correlate karma vs XP; high correlation falsifies. Eyeball grant graph for reciprocity clusters/new-account spikes. Cheapest: time-boxed thin-slice trial in one real server with logging.
## (4) Cost vs payoff
Core mechanic cheap (reuses XP/coins leaderboard scaffolding); expensive part is integrity (caps, cooldowns, self/alt blocking, reciprocity dampening, sybil resistance) - all not measured. Payoff near-zero if correlated.
## (5) Smallest slice
Single grant command with basic guards only: no self-grant, per-pair cooldown, small per-granter daily cap, purely reputational (non-spendable). Ship read-only-scored with its own leaderboard, log every grant, run correlation-vs-XP after a trial window before deeper anti-sybil work.
